events.title: Mickalene Thomas:All About Love
events.description: The first major international tour on Thomas’s pioneering work.

events.title: Cecily Brown: Themes and Variations
events.description: Surveying three decades of the British painter’s career.

events.title: From Paris to Provence: French Painting at the Barnes
events.description: Travel through France with Monet, Van Gogh, Cézanne, and other trailblazing painters.

events.title: Where I’m From
events.description: Works created by artists from Mural Arts Philadelphia’s Rec Crew and SCI Phoenix.

events.title: The Battle of the Bathers
events.description: Learn about the controversy between Dr. Barnes and the Philadelphia Museum of Art around works by Paul Cézanne.

events.title: Henri Rousseau: A Painter’s Secrets
events.description: See the inventive works of Henri Rousseau in a new light.

events.title: Revolutionary Botany
events.description: Tracing the evolution of medicine in America from medicinal plants to the modern pharmacy. Part of the Mütter Museum's 2026 programming celebrating medical milestones throughout the past 250 years.

events.title: Noah Davis
events.description: A landmark survey bringing together over 60 works charting Noah Davis's (1983-2015) practice. Davis's luminous paintings blend figuration and abstraction, drawing on art history and personal memory.

events.title: Universal Theme Parks: The Exhibition
events.description: World premiere spanning 18,000 square feet with eight themed galleries, 25 interactive experiences, and over 100 original artifacts from Universal's iconic theme parks. Extended evening hours Thu-Sat.

events.title: Ancient Egypt in Watercolors: Paintings and Artifacts from Dra Abu el-Naga
events.description: Century-old watercolors by Egyptian artist Ahmed Yousef capturing New Kingdom tomb chapels from Dra Abu el-Naga, Thebes. First rotation through June; second rotation from July through November ahead of the Egypt Galleries grand reopening.

events.title: A Delicate Balance
events.description: Edward Albee's Pulitzer Prize-winning play — an absorbing look at the everyday hopes, fears, and secrets we all delicately balance. America's oldest theater.

events.title: Romeo and Juliet
events.description: A bold, contemporary reimagining of Shakespeare's most famous love story. The Arden Theatre strips the tragedy down to its raw emotional core — passion, family loyalty, and the devastating cost of hatred. Intimate staging in Old City.

events.title: The Most Spectacularly Lamentable Trial of Miz Martha Washington
events.description: From Pulitzer Prize winner James Ijames — First Lady Martha Washington faces the enslaved people who will be freed upon her death. HotHouse Acting Company.

events.title: Scratch Nights
events.description: 140 N. Columbus Blvd. (at Race St.) Philadelphia, PA 19106 Directions

events.title: BalletX Spring Series 2026
events.description: BalletX celebrates its 20th anniversary with three works by Matthew Neenan — Show Me, Broke Apart, and more.

events.title: Endea Owens & The Cookout
events.description: Grammy, Emmy, and Peabody Award winner, Endea Owens is hailed as one of jazz's most vibrant and versatile artists. The Detroit-raised recording artist, bassist, and composer has been mentored by jazz icons like Marcus Belgrave, Rodney Whitaker, and Ron Carter.

events.title: Sofar Sounds Philadelphia: Secret Concert
events.description: Intimate live music in a secret Philadelphia location, revealed 24 hours before the show. Three emerging artists perform acoustic sets in an unplugged, phone-free environment. Sofar reimagines the live music experience.

events.title: Lang Lang and Yannick
events.description: There are artists who seize the public imagination, and perhaps none has done so as spectacularly as Lang Lang. Whether he’s performing for billions, as at the 2008 Olympics Opening Ceremony, or for a small group of school children, his artistry and passion is on full display.

events.title: Between the Notes: Sleepers Awake Virtual Lecture
events.description: Between the Notes: Sleepers Awake Virtual Lecture 12:00 p.m. | Zoom Available to Opera Philadelphia Subscribers and Contributor level donors ($100) and above

events.title: We Get Requests
events.description: We Get Requests is an engaging live music night led by Philadelphia trumpeter and GRAMMY-nominated artist Josh Lawrence. Each performance features Lawrence and a rotating trio of some of Philadelphia’s most respected musicians, with special guest artists joining the second set. Audience members can submit song requests in person or online between sets, shaping a spontaneous and memorable evening where the band brings those selections to life on stage.Set times 7:30pm & 9:00pm General Admission ~

events.title: Lang Lang and Yannick
events.description: A Benefit Concert for the Musicians' Retirement Fund. The celebrated pianist Lang Lang joins Yannick Nézet-Séguin and the Orchestra in Beethoven's lyrical Piano Concerto No. 4. A one-night-only engagement.

events.title: Between the Notes: Sleepers Awake Virtual Lecture
events.description: A free virtual lecture exploring the themes and music behind Opera Philadelphia's world premiere Sleepers Awake. Join the conversation with the creative team as they discuss the making of this groundbreaking new work by Gregory Spears.

events.title: The Stinky Cheese Man and Other Fairly Stupid Tales

events.title: Mozart’s Requiem
events.description: A masterwork from a prodigiously gifted composer who shaped the music of his lifetime and centuries thereafter. In his Requiem, Mozart summoned all the fire and brimstone hell could hold, making ominously real the awful majesty of death and the miracle of redemption.

events.description: How will artificial intelligence reshape the job market? A technology researcher explores the economic and social implications of AI automation, and what it means for workers today.

events.title: The Black Clown (Philadelphia Premiere)
events.description: Philadelphia premiere — a genre-shattering work based on Langston Hughes' searing 1931 poem. Fuses vaudeville, gospel, opera, jazz, and spirituals to tell the story of Black joy and perseverance against impossible odds. A visceral theatrical experience.

events.title: Betz–Gillece Quartet
events.description: The Betz–Gillece Quartet brings together two of the region’s most distinctive jazz voices—guitarist Brian Betz and vibraphonist Behn Gillece—for an evening of original compositions and fresh arrangements. Known for their long-standing musical partnership, Betz and Gillece blend lyrical melodies, modern harmony, and dynamic interplay, supported by an exceptional rhythm section featuring bassist Charles Ford and drummer Dan Monaghan. The group’s sound reflects a balance of swing, groove, and conte

events.title: The Barnes Collection
events.description: See art differently. Explore paintings by Renoir, Cézanne, Matisse, Picasso, Modigliani, Van Gogh, and other artists beside African sculpture, metalwork, and decorative arts.

events.title: Highlights Tour
events.description: This one-hour tour is perfect for first-time visitors. Explore highlights of the collection and learn the history of the Barnes.

events.title: Private Collection Tour
events.description: Explore highlights of the collection and learn the history of the Barnes. This tour is a private one-hour experience for you and up to nine guests.

events.title: Private Collection Tour: Favorite Artists
events.description: Choose your favorite artists in the collection for a private one-hour experience for up to ten guests.

events.title: Daily Gallery Talks
events.description: Every day, our gallery specialists offer talks about the Barnes collection. Learn more about our history, founder, and collection.

events.title: April Spotlight Tour: Matisse, Dr. Barnes, and The Dance
events.description: Tour our collection of paintings by Henri Matisse and learn how a visit to the Barnes collection was a pivotal moment in the artist's career.

events.title: March Spotlight Tour: Matisse, Dr. Barnes, and The Dance
events.description: Tour our collection of paintings by Henri Matisse and learn how a visit to the Barnes collection was a pivotal moment in the artist's career.

events.title: The Barnes Collection
events.description: See art differently. Explore paintings by Renoir, Cézanne, Matisse, Picasso, Modigliani, Van Gogh, and other artists beside African sculpture, metalwork, and decorative arts.

events.title: The Franklin Institute is open Saturday, March 28. Road closures in Philadelphia may impact travel to the museum, please plan your route and allow for extra travel time.
The Franklin Institute is open Saturday, March 28. Road closures in Philadelphia may impact travel to the museum, please plan your route and allow for extra travel time.
Exhibits & Experiences
events.description: Play your way through eight themed galleries, where 100 original artifacts bring each experience to life. From authentic costumes and iconic props to early concept models, experience the creativity, artistry, and innovation behind Universal’s most epic attractions.

events.title: Universal Theme Parks: The Exhibition
events.description: Play your way through eight themed galleries, where 100 original artifacts bring each experience to life. From authentic costumes and iconic props to early concept models, experience the creativity, artistry, and innovation behind Universal’s most epic attractions.

events.title: Wondrous Space
events.description: Journey through the cosmos as you explore this immersive, multisensory, two-story exhibit designed to inspire an excitement for space science.

events.title: Body Odyssey
events.description: Curiosity meets discovery in a new exhibit surrounding the iconic Giant Heart. A journey through the human body in a quest to achieve balance in life and well-being.

events.title: Hamilton Collections Gallery
events.description: Surrounding the Baldwin 60000, guests will discover a treasure trove of rare artifacts meticulously curated from The Franklin Institute's collections.

events.title: Your Brain
events.description: Think about how you think when you explore Your Brain. What is the brain? How does it work?

events.title: SportsZone
events.description: Hit the turf and experience how sports connect the science of the human body, laws of motion, and technical innovation.

events.title: Sir Isaac's Loft
events.description: Enter the playground of experimentation and discover falling objects, chain reactions, and optical illusions that bring Newton's laws to life.

events.title: Amazing Machine
events.description: Explore the amazing world of machines! You’ll be amazed at the similarities between the machines from our collection and the ones you use every day.

events.title: Brick Play Space Pop-Up
events.description: Click, snap, and stack LEGO bricks at three interactive stations! Create LEGO graffiti, build a race car, and follow fun blueprints.

events.title: Benjamin Franklin Memorial
events.description: The Benjamin Franklin National Memorial features a dramatic 20-foot-high marble statue of Benjamin Franklin sculpted by James Earle Fraser.

events.title: The Holt & Miller Observatory
events.description: The Holt & Miller Observatory houses the 10” Carl Zeiss Refracting Telescope, one of the largest historical artifacts in the museum's collection.

events.title: Daily Live Science Demonstrations
events.description: Experience the thrill of live science as our science performers bring scientific concepts to life through captivating demonstrations and interactive experiments.

events.title: Fels Planetarium
events.description: Explore the night sky with cutting-edge astronomical presentations in the awe-inspiring Fels Planetarium, presented by PNC Bank.

events.title: Seasonal Events
events.description: Step into a world of discovery at The Franklin Institute, where every season brings new adventures for the whole family!

events.title: Science After Hours
events.description: Science After Hours is an evening event series exclusively for adults ages 21+, featuring experiments, demonstrations, games, and engaging science curated in collaboration with cultural organizations across the city.

events.title: Africa
events.description: Africa Connect with the great kingdoms and rich material culture of Africa through 300 objects from 21 countries.

events.title: Ancient Egypt & Nubia
events.description: Ancient Egypt & Nubia Explore one of the largest collections of Egyptian artifacts in the United States.

events.title: Asia
events.description: Asia Marvel at one of the finest collections of monumental Asian art showcased in the architecturally stunning 90-foot tall Rotunda.

events.title: Eastern Mediterranean
events.description: Eastern Mediterranean Unearth an ancient crossroads of diverse cultures whose innovations shaped our world today.

events.title: Etruscan Italy
events.description: Etruscan Italy Learn about the Etruscan people, the first great rulers of central Italy from around 800 to 100 BCE, through the gems, statues, weaponry, jewelry, and terracotta vessels they left behind.

events.title: Greece
events.description: Greece Explore the far reaches of Alexander the Great’s vast empire through coins, pottery, and statues.

events.title: Mexico & Central America
events.description: Mexico & Central America Experience the vibrant heritage and diverse cultures of the ancient Americas.

events.title: Middle East
events.description: Middle East Trace the story of how ancient Mesopotamian societies gave rise to the world's first cities

events.title: Native North America
events.description: Native North America Gain compelling first-person perspectives through this gallery, which draws together archaeological, ethnographic, and contemporary works of technology and art to highlight cultural continuity, creativity, and resilience through time.

events.title: Rome
events.description: Rome Come face to face with the people of the ancient Roman empire through artifacts ranging from miniature glass vessels to large marble reliefs.
